overview: Izzet Spells is a tempo strategy that leverages cheap, synergistic instants and sorceries to establish early board presence while generating card advantage. By cycling through the deck rapidly, the pilot aims to outpace opponents with consistent value generation and disruptive interaction. The deck relies on spell-triggered effects to maintain pressure and control the pace of the game.
win_conditions: Casting a high volume of spells to trigger draw effects
Developing a resilient board presence with spell-activated creatures
Overwhelming opponents with cumulative value and direct damage

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more cheap spells
Hands containing a signature card paired with interaction
Hands that can play a turn-one spell or creature
Keep hands with 2-3 lands and at least one spell-casting engine (Flow State, Slickshot Show-Off, or a cheap cantrip).
Keep hands with multiple cheap instants/sorceries (1-2 mana) that enable Stormchaser's Talent or Show-Off.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or card draw
Hands missing land drops for the first three turns
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, three+ lands with no early spells, or hands lacking any way to cast multiple spells in one turn.

early_game: Play cheap spells to trigger Stormchaser's Talent and establish early tempo
Deploy Slickshot Show-Off to apply pressure and enable spell synergies
Hold interaction for opponent's early threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or cheap spell to set up the turn.
If you have Flow State, play it on turn 1 or 2 if you have mana to cast a spell from your library.
Turn 2: Cast a second spell, ideally a cantrip or removal, to build spell count.
If you have Slickshot Show-Off, cast it on turn 2 or 3 after casting 1-2 spells to make it a meaningful threat.
Always prioritize casting spells over holding up mana for counterspells unless facing a known control deck.
mid_game: Cast Flow State to accelerate card advantage and stabilize the board
Use removal and counterspells to protect key permanents
Cycle through the deck to find finishers and additional value engines
Treat your library as a secondary hand when Flow State is active.
Prioritize casting instants/sorceries over creatures to maximize spell count for Stormchaser's Talent or Show-Off.
Don't overextend creatures if you can win by casting spells.
Manage your mana to ensure you can cast your win condition in a single turn
Commit creatures like Slickshot Show-Off only when you can protect them or when you're ready to swing for lethal.
late_game: Leverage accumulated card advantage to out-resource opponents
Maintain board control while chipping away at life totals
Commit to all-in spells when the opponent runs out of answers
Stormchaser's Talent is the primary finisher
cast it after 3+ spells in a turn for lethal.
Slickshot Show-Off can close the game if it's large enough and unblocked.
Flow State enables infinite or semi-infinite spell chains if paired with draw engines or cheap cantrips.
Go all-in when you have Flow State on the battlefield, enough mana to cast 4+ spells, and a clear path to lethal.
Also commit when the opponent has no interaction left or is at low life.

key_cards.name: Stormchaser's Talent
key_cards.role: Generates card advantage when spells are cast
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting it early to fuel the deck's spell synergy and maintain hand size

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.role: Provides early board presence and spell synergy
key_cards.notes: Use as a turn-one or turn-two play to establish tempo and trigger spell-based effects

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.role: Rewards casting multiple spells with value or draw
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have a sequence of spells ready to maximize its trigger potential and stabilize the game

sequencing_tips: Hold up interaction until the opponent commits to a threat
Cast cheap spells before playing permanents to maximize trigger counts
Prioritize drawing cards over dealing damage when behind on tempo
Save removal for high-impact threats rather than early creatures
never cast it unless you can immediately cast an instant or sorcery from your library.
Stormchaser's Talent scales with the number of spells cast this turn, so sequence your spells carefully (e.g., cantrip -> removal -> Stormchaser's Talent).
Slickshot Show-Off is vulnerable to sweepers
don't rely on it as your only win condition.
Watch out for spell count math

matchups.advice: Focus on early disruption and card draw to outlast the initial rush
Apply consistent pressure and use spell synergy to generate value faster than their answers
Race them with efficient spells while protecting your key permanents

Use cheap spells to trade with counters. Cast your win condition in response to their spells, or use multiple spells to force multiple counters. Bring in more cantrips or discard to remove their counterspells.

Apply early pressure with Slickshot Show-Off and cantrips. Don't let them get to their big mana; use spot removal and discard to disrupt their land drops. If they ramp, win quickly with Stormchaser's Talent before they stabilize.

Win the spell war by having better sequencing and more interaction. Use discard to remove their Flow State or Stormchaser's Talent. Hold up counterspells for their win conditions. Prioritize casting spells in response to their spells to maximize your own spell count.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (hold up mana or cast in response to their spells), sweepers (have a way to protect your win condition or win before it resolves), and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells
that's your window to cast multiple spells.
